title: Rich Slovakian Potato Chowder
description: A creamy, herbal chowder with dark soy sauce and fermented dough, served from a salt-block for a warming, crowd-pleasing experience.
instructions: Heat a large salt-block skillet over medium heat and add a light drizzle of oil.
Sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots, chopped celery, and diced potatoes, cooking for another 5 minutes while stirring.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer, allowing the flavors to meld for 15 minutes.
Stir in the shredded long-fermentation-dough, dark soy sauce, cumin, coriander, dill, and parsley, then cover and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and add the heavy cream, stirring until the chowder thickens slightly, about 5 minutes.
Season with black pepper and salt to taste, adjusting the balance of savory and umami.
Transfer the chowder to a serving bowl, letting it rest on the salt block to keep it warm.
Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh parsley and a small drizzle of soy sauce if desired.
Serve hot, ensuring guests experience the creamy, herbal depth of this Slovakian-inspired dish.
